<div align="center">

# 📊 Customer Churn Prediction API

**An end-to-end machine learning system that predicts telecom customer churn and serves live predictions through a production-ready REST API.**

[![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.12-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/)
[![FastAPI](https://img.shields.io/badge/FastAPI-0.139-009688?logo=fastapi&logoColor=white)](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/)
[![XGBoost](https://img.shields.io/badge/Model-XGBoost-EB5E28)](https://xgboost.readthedocs.io/)
[![Docker](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docker-Ready-2496ED?logo=docker&logoColor=white)](https://www.docker.com/)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Educational-lightgrey)](#-license)

[Overview](#-overview) • [How It Works](#-how-it-works) • [API Reference](#-api-reference) • [Quick Start](#-quick-start) • [Model Performance](#-model-performance) • [Roadmap](#-roadmap)

</div>

---

## 🌐 Live Demo

The API is deployed and publicly reachable on **Render**:

🔗 **Base URL:** [`https://customer-churn-prediction-jmiu.onrender.com`](https://customer-churn-prediction-jmiu.onrender.com)
📖 **Interactive Docs (Swagger UI):** [`/docs`](https://customer-churn-prediction-jmiu.onrender.com/docs)

> Render's free tier spins the service down after inactivity, so the first request after a period of idleness may take a few seconds while it wakes up.

---

## 📌 Overview

Customer churn — when a subscriber stops using a company's service — is one of the costliest problems in subscription-based businesses. Retaining an existing customer is significantly cheaper than acquiring a new one, which makes **early churn detection** a high-value use case for machine learning.

This project trains a classifier on customer demographic, account, and service-usage data, then wraps it in a lightweight, containerized **FastAPI** service so the prediction can be consumed by any application — a web dashboard, a CRM, or a batch job — with a single HTTP call.

**What it does:**
- 🔍 Takes a customer's profile (contract type, tenure, billing, subscribed services, etc.)
- 🧠 Runs it through a trained XGBoost model
- 📤 Returns a `Churn` / `No Churn` label plus a probability score

---

## 🧱 How It Works

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[churn_dataset.csv] --> B[Clean & Encode\ntrain.py]
    B --> C[Scale Features\nStandardScaler]
    C --> D[Balance Classes\nSMOTE]
    D --> E[Train Model\nXGBoost]
    E --> F[(churn_model.pkl\nscaler.pkl\nfeature_names.pkl)]
    F --> G[FastAPI Service\napp.py]
    G --> H[POST /predict]
    H --> I[Churn Prediction\n+ Probability]
```

The API applies the **exact same preprocessing** used during training (one-hot encoding → column alignment → scaling) before calling the model, ensuring training/serving consistency.

---

## 🗂️ Project Structure

```
Customer_Churn_Project/
│
├── app.py                # FastAPI service — loads artifacts and serves predictions
├── train.py               # Preprocessing, SMOTE balancing, model training & evaluation
├── churn_dataset.csv      # Source dataset
├── churn_model.pkl        # Trained XGBoost model (artifact)
├── scaler.pkl             # Fitted StandardScaler (artifact)
├── feature_names.pkl      # Training-time feature order (artifact)
├── requirements.txt        # Pinned Python dependencies
├── Dockerfile              # Container build definition
└── README.md
```

---

## 📚 Dataset

`churn_dataset.csv` contains one row per customer:

| Category | Fields |
|---|---|
| **Identifier** | `customerID` |
| **Demographics** | `gender`, `SeniorCitizen`, `Partner`, `Dependents` |
| **Account** | `tenure`, `Contract`, `PaperlessBilling`, `PaymentMethod`, `MonthlyCharges`, `TotalCharges` |
| **Services** | `PhoneService`, `MultipleLines`, `InternetService`, `OnlineSecurity`, `OnlineBackup`, `DeviceProtection`, `TechSupport`, `StreamingTV`, `StreamingMovies` |
| **Target** | `Churn` (`Yes` / `No`) |

---

## ⚙️ Machine Learning Pipeline

| Step | Technique | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **1. Cleaning** | Drop `customerID`, coerce `TotalCharges` to numeric, drop nulls | Remove noise and non-predictive fields |
| **2. Encoding** | `pd.get_dummies(drop_first=True)` | Convert categorical fields to numeric |
| **3. Split** | 80/20, stratified | Preserve class ratio in train/test sets |
| **4. Scaling** | `StandardScaler` | Normalize feature magnitude |
| **5. Balancing** | `SMOTE` | Correct churn/non-churn class imbalance |
| **6. Training** | `XGBClassifier` (`n_estimators=50`, `max_depth=4`, `lr=0.1`) | Learn churn patterns |
| **7. Experiment Tracking** | `MLflow` | Log and compare training runs across iterations |
| **8. Export** | `joblib.dump(...)` | Persist model, scaler, and feature schema for serving |

Training has been iterated on extensively during development — the project's MLflow tracking server has logged **100+ runs** while tuning preprocessing and model parameters (see [screenshots](#-screenshots) below).

---

## 📈 Model Performance

Measured on a held-out 20% test split (60 customers):

| Metric | Score |
|---|---|
| Accuracy | **0.47** |
| Precision (Churn) | 0.42 |
| Recall (Churn) | 0.39 |
| F1-score (Churn) | 0.41 |

> **⚠️ Honest note:** The bundled dataset has only 300 rows, which is too small for the model to learn reliable patterns — current performance is close to chance level. This is called out explicitly rather than glossed over, and is the top priority in the [Roadmap](#-roadmap) below. Swapping in a larger, real-world dataset (e.g. the [IBM Telco Customer Churn dataset](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/blastchar/telco-customer-churn), ~7,000 rows) is the single highest-impact next step.

---

## 🌐 API Reference

Interactive Swagger docs are auto-generated at **`/docs`** once the server is running.

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/` | Confirms the API is running |
| `GET` | `/health` | Reports whether the model/scaler loaded correctly |
| `POST` | `/predict` | Accepts a customer profile, returns a churn prediction |

<details>
<summary><strong>▶ Example request</strong></summary>

```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8000/predict" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "gender": "Female",
    "SeniorCitizen": 0,
    "Partner": "Yes",
    "Dependents": "No",
    "tenure": 12,
    "PhoneService": "Yes",
    "MultipleLines": "No",
    "InternetService": "DSL",
    "OnlineSecurity": "Yes",
    "OnlineBackup": "No",
    "DeviceProtection": "No",
    "TechSupport": "No",
    "StreamingTV": "No",
    "StreamingMovies": "No",
    "Contract": "Month-to-month",
    "PaperlessBilling": "Yes",
    "PaymentMethod": "Electronic check",
    "MonthlyCharges": 55.5,
    "TotalCharges": 660.0
  }'
```

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>▶ Example response</strong></summary>

```json
{
  "prediction": "Churn",
  "churn_probability": 0.812
}
```

</details>

---

---

## 🛠️ Tech Stack

| Layer | Tools |
|---|---|
| **Language** | Python 3.12 |
| **Modeling** | XGBoost, scikit-learn, imbalanced-learn (SMOTE) |
| **Experiment tracking** | MLflow |
| **Data handling** | Pandas, NumPy |
| **API** | FastAPI, Uvicorn, Gunicorn |
| **Packaging** | Docker |
| **Deployment** | Render |

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### 1. Clone & enter the project
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Maruthireddy642/Customer_Churn_Project.git
cd Customer_Churn_Project
```

### 2. Set up a virtual environment
```bash
# Linux/Mac
python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate

# Windows
python -m venv .venv && .venv\Scripts\activate
```

### 3. Install dependencies
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 4. (Optional) Retrain the model
```bash
python train.py
```

### 5. Launch the API
```bash
uvicorn app:app --reload
```
Visit **http://localhost:8000/docs** to try it out interactively.

---

## 🐳 Docker

```bash
# Build
docker build -t customer-churn-api .

# Run
docker run -p 8000:8000 customer-churn-api
```

API available at **http://localhost:8000**.

---

## 🗺️ Roadmap

- [ ] Replace the 300-row sample dataset with a larger, real-world dataset to materially improve model performance
- [ ] Add hyperparameter tuning (GridSearchCV / Optuna) and cross-validation
- [x] Track experiments with MLflow — 100+ runs logged during development
- [ ] Add SHAP-based explainability to `/predict` responses
- [ ] Add automated tests + CI/CD pipeline
- [x] Deploy to a public endpoint with a live demo link — see [Live Demo](#-live-demo)
- [ ] Build a simple front-end (Streamlit) for non-technical users
- [ ] Register best MLflow run as the canonical served model, instead of a manually exported `.pkl`
